rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF ALLEN ROGERS SIMPSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #3, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/25/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Original Probate Code Section 13100 affidavits must be on file. [LRSF 14.47 F.5.d.] (2) Bond should be reduced to $123,000. (10% of $111,310 = $11,131.) Please provide a courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 6/11/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (The conservators -- with their names misspelled -- were appointed on 10/31/19, not 10/13/19. Ms. Palmer was discharged by the order dated 6/9/22 and filed on 6/10/22. The order settling the second account was dated 4/11/23 and filed on 4/12/23.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DAVID FRANK PHILLIPS II
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/8/25 (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (2) No bank statements have been filed to verify the ending balance(s) shown in the accounting. [LRSF 14.34 D.] See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. (3) Please file a copy of the seller's closing statement to verify the costs of sale. (4) The accounting does not begin with the correct total of the assets on hand as of 12/31/21. The balance on hand in the first account, as amended, showed assets totaling $2,812,331.85 at their carry value. (This accounting also shows the same FMV of the real property, although the first account showed that FMV to be $1,900,000.) (5) Receipts for prior distributions must be filed. [LRSF 14.35 I.6.c.] There was an order for preliminary distribution signed and filed on 11/23/22. (6) Reimbursements to any individual must indicate who the original payee(s) was/were. (7) A petition for final distribution must also state the date on which notice was mailed to the FTB. [LRSF 14.35 I.2.] (8) The prior attorney's costs requested now include $450 for a filing fee on 5/26/22, but the filing fee for that first accounting was $435. There was also no need to provide each interested party with a copy of the document costing nearly $650; notice of hearing is sufficient. There was no declaration by the executor filed on 8/18/22 certifying the retention of original documents. (9) Payments to a third party to prepare the accounting must be deducted from the executor's statutory compensation. (10) The statutory fee calculation has a mathematical error. (11) Far more than the FDIC insured amount is being held at one bank. Please provide a proposed order (if applicable) and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 6/24/25. (Paragraph 32 of the amended petition is rather confusing and does not appear to be supported by the accounting, which shows assets totaling $2,471,622.19 on hand as of 12/31/24.) Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF JENNY LEA BROWNSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/25/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: 1. An amended second account was just filed on 5/1/25, which was not at least 15 days before this hearing. (Notice may not be mailed before the pleading requiring notice is e-filed. [LRSF 14.14 A.]) 2. When an accounting is presented, original bank or brokerage statements must be filed to verify the ending balance(s) shown in the accounting. None appears to have been filed. [LRSF 14.34 D.] See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. 3. No courtesy copy of the new pleading has been received yet by the examiner, but a cursory review of it indicates that distribution of the remaining assets on hand is open-ended. The conservator must disclose specifically how the assets will be distributed. [LRSF 14.47 F.5.] Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (An amended petition fully restates and supersedes the pleading it amends; it should stand on its own and not refer to (address) issues raised by an examiner, which issues are not part of the record.)

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE WENTZ FAMILY LIVING TRUST DATED MARCH 14, 2011
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order To Compel Mediation
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required, although notice was defective. (Instructions for how to appear remotely were not attached to the notice. A separately filed "Notice of Directions for Remote Appearance and Access" filed on 5/1/25 indicates that the instructions alone, without a notice of hearing, were mailed on 5/1/25, which was not at least 30 days before this hearing. If a pleading form of proof of service is used in probate and trust proceedings, a copy of the NOTICE OF HEARING that was served, showing what is to be heard and when, etc., must be attached.) Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DORIS TAYLOR
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION TO DETERMINE SUCCESSION TO REAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/1/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) How can the court find and order that Aisha Flenoid is the sole heir entitled to this asset, when there are other heirs who have not disclaimed their interests? (2) Lenda Taylor post-deceased Doris Taylor. If she died intestate and no estate was opened for her, Isaiah Taylor's affidavit pursuant to Probate Code Section 13100 must be filed to affirm that he would have been the only person entitled to her estate. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 6/17/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF PHILLIP LEW
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/17/25. The trustee of the devisee trust is not identified in item 8 of the petition; if it is the petitioner, all trust beneficiaries must also be listed and identified as such. (The will waived bond; waivers of bond were not necessary.) A courtesy copy of any papers filed to address this issue (a supplement, signed and verified by the petitioner, and proof of service, if applicable) must be received by the assigned examiner by no later than 6/3/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
